rag.description: Delete a Google Calendar event by its unique identifier from a target calendar (defaults to primary) during an automation run, using either a direct OAuth token or a stored credentials reference. Supports attendee notification control via SendUpdates. Fails with a non-success API response when the calendar or event cannot be found.

qa.question: What does the RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent plugin do?
qa.answer: It deletes a Google Calendar event by its unique identifier using the Calendar API v3 events.delete endpoint.

qa.question: What parameters does RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent require?
qa.answer: EventId is required. CalendarId defaults to 'primary'. SendUpdates is optional and defaults to 'none'. Authentication uses Token or Credentials (Credentials takes precedence).

qa.question: How does RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent notify attendees of the deletion?
qa.answer: Pass SendUpdates:all to notify all attendees, SendUpdates:externalOnly to notify only external attendees, or SendUpdates:none (the default) to send no notifications.

qa.question: How does RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent fit into the integration context of an automation workflow?
qa.answer: It runs as an Action in a sequential workflow step (componentType 'task') using the google icon provider and the ActionRuleModel.

qa.question: What are best practices for using RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent in automations?
qa.answer: Prefer Credentials over a raw Token for long-running runs to allow token refresh. Use SendUpdates:none in test runs to avoid sending spurious cancellation emails. Pass CalendarId explicitly when targeting a secondary calendar.

qa.question: What rule or schema details should I follow when configuring RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent?
qa.answer: Use $type 'Action' with pluginName 'RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent' and pass at minimum EventId via argument.

qa.question: What is the manifest key of this plugin?
What manifestVersion is used by this plugin?
What is the pluginType of RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent?
Which platforms are supported by this plugin?
Which categories is this plugin associated with?
qa.answer: The plugin is listed under the 'GoogleCalendar' category.

qa.question: Who is the author of this plugin manifest?
What is the summary of RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent?
qa.answer: Deletes a Google Calendar event by its identifier from a target calendar, with optional attendee notification control.

qa.question: How does RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent handle errors and edge cases?
qa.answer: It fails with a non-success API response when the event or calendar is not found (404), the token is invalid or expired (401), the authenticated user lacks write access (403), or the Google Calendar API returns a non-success status code for any other reason.

description: ### Purpose
Deletes a Google Calendar event by its unique identifier from the target calendar.
Targets the authenticated user's primary calendar by default when CalendarId is omitted.
Authenticates via a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference exchanged for a fresh token.
Controls whether attendees are notified of the deletion via SendUpdates.
### Key Features and Functionality
| Feature                    | Description                                                                                           |
| Delete event               | Deletes an event by id using the Google Calendar API (Events: delete).                                |
| Calendar targeting         | Targets any accessible calendar by CalendarId; defaults to the authenticated user's primary calendar. |
| Notification control       | Controls whether attendees are notified of the deletion via SendUpdates.                              |
| Token authentication       | Accepts a raw OAuth Bearer access token.                                                              |
| Credentials authentication | Accepts a credentials id/name and exchanges it for a fresh access token.                              |
### Usages in RPA
| Use Case                             | Description                                                              |
| Cancel scheduled meetings            | Delete events that are no longer needed after a workflow decision.       |
| Clean up test events                 | Remove events created during automated testing runs.                     |
| Sync deletions from external systems | Mirror event cancellations from an external system into Google Calendar. |
| Remove outdated calendar blocks      | Delete focus time or out-of-office blocks when conditions change.        |

edge_cases: EventId is missing or empty
EventId does not exist on the target calendar — API returns 404
CalendarId defaults to 'primary' when omitted
Invalid or expired OAuth access token — API returns 401
Access token missing required Google Calendar API scope
SendUpdates defaults to 'none' — no cancellation emails are sent
Google Calendar API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure
annotations.expected_result: The event is deleted from the primary calendar. The API returns 204 No Content.
annotations.notes: Deletes an event from the primary calendar using a direct OAuth access token.

description: ### RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent: Delete event from primary calendar using access token
Delete a calendar event by its unique identifier from the primary calendar.
CalendarId is omitted so the plugin targets the authenticated user's primary calendar.
SendUpdates is omitted so no cancellation emails are sent to attendees.

edge_cases: Credentials id/name not found
Credentials record missing refresh token
Refresh-token exchange fails
Google OAuth token endpoint returns non-success status code
EventId does not exist on the target calendar — API returns 404
CalendarId not found — API returns 404
CalendarId refers to a calendar the user cannot write to — API returns 403
CalendarId contains special characters that require URL encoding
SendUpdates:all causes cancellation emails to be sent to all attendees immediately
Google Calendar API rate limit or quota exceeded
Google Calendar API returns non-success status code
Network timeout or connection failure
annotations.expected_result: The event is deleted from the specified secondary calendar and cancellation emails are sent to all attendees. The API returns 204 No Content.
annotations.notes: Deletes an event from a secondary calendar using stored credentials, notifying all attendees.

description: ### RemoveGoogleCalendarEvent: Delete event from secondary calendar using stored credentials
Delete a calendar event from a secondary calendar by supplying its identifier and CalendarId.
Stored credentials are resolved at runtime to obtain a fresh OAuth access token.
SendUpdates:all notifies all attendees of the cancellation.
Unique identifier of the calendar that contains the event to delete.
Defaults to 'primary' when not provided, targeting the authenticated user's primary calendar.
Any valid calendar identifier is accepted, including secondary calendar IDs in email-address format.
parameters.name: CalendarId

description: Stored credential record id or name used to obtain a fresh access token.
Avoids hardcoding tokens by exchanging the stored refresh token for a new access token when needed.
Use when access tokens may expire between steps.
Takes precedence over the Token parameter when both are provided.
parameters.name: Credentials

description: Unique identifier of the event to delete.
Use the id returned by NewGoogleCalendarEvent or retrieved from the Calendar API.
parameters.name: EventId

description: Controls whether cancellation emails are sent to attendees when the event is deleted.
Accepted values: 'all' (notify all attendees), 'externalOnly' (notify only external attendees), 'none' (send no notifications).
Defaults to 'none' to avoid sending spurious emails during automated runs.
parameters.name: SendUpdates

description: OAuth Bearer access token used to call the Google Calendar API.
Used when no stored credential record is available.
Ignored when Credentials is provided.
parameters.name: Token

summary: Deletes a Google Calendar event by its unique identifier from the target calendar.
Authenticates via a direct OAuth access token or a stored credentials reference exchanged for a fresh token.
Fails with a non-success API response when the calendar or event cannot be found.
